Description: Never hardcode sensitive values in Terraform code
Do: Use variables, environment variables, or secrets manager for sensitive values
Don't: Hardcode passwords, API keys, or tokens in .tf files
Code Example Good: variable "db_password" { type = string sensitive = true } resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { password = var.db_password }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { password = "MyP@ssw0rd123" }
Remediation: Move secrets to variables with sensitive=true or use AWS Secrets Manager/HashiCorp Vault

Description: Use dedicated secrets management services
Do: Store secrets in AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or Parameter Store
Don't: Store secrets in environment variables or plain text files
Code Example Good: data "aws_secretsmanager_secret_version" "db" { secret_id = aws_secretsmanager_secret.db.id } resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { password = data.aws_secretsmanager_secret_version.db.secret_string }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { password = var.db_password # from env var }
Remediation: Migrate secrets to secrets manager, update references

Description: Use minimum required permissions for IAM policies
Do: Define specific actions, resources, and conditions
Don't: Use wildcard (*) permissions or overly broad policies
Code Example Good: { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject"], "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/*" }
Code Example Bad: { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "*", "Resource": "*" }
Remediation: Review and restrict IAM policies to specific resources and actions

Description: Never create access keys for root account
Do: Use IAM users or roles with appropriate permissions
Don't: Create or use root account access keys
Code Example Good: resource "aws_iam_user" "admin" { name = "admin" } resource "aws_iam_user_policy_attachment" "admin" { user = aws_iam_user.admin.name policy_arn = "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess" }
Code Example Bad: # Using root credentials in provider - NEVER DO THIS provider "aws" { access_key = "AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE" secret_key = "wJalrXUtnFEMI..." }
Remediation: Delete root access keys, create IAM admin user with MFA

Description: Enable MFA for IAM users and sensitive operations
Do: Require MFA for console access and sensitive API calls
Don't: Allow access without MFA for admin operations
Code Example Good: resource "aws_iam_user_login_profile" "admin" { user = aws_iam_user.admin.name } # MFA enforced via policy condition: "Condition": { "Bool": { "aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent": "true" } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_iam_user" "admin" { name = "admin" } # No MFA requirement
Remediation: Create MFA devices for users, add MFA condition to policies

Description: Enable encryption for all data at rest
Do: Enable encryption on S3, RDS, EBS, and all storage services
Don't: Leave storage resources unencrypted
Code Example Good: resource "aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration" "encrypted" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id rule { apply_server_side_encryption_by_default { sse_algorithm = "aws:kms" kms_master_key_id = aws_kms_key.main.arn } } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" { bucket = "my-bucket" # No encryption configured }
Remediation: Enable encryption with SSE-KMS or SSE-S3

Keywords: encryption, transit, tls, ssl, https, network
Description: Use TLS/SSL for all data in transit
Do: Enforce HTTPS, use TLS 1.2+, configure secure listeners
Don't: Allow unencrypted HTTP traffic for sensitive data
Code Example Good: resource "aws_lb_listener" "https" { load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.main.arn port = 443 protocol = "HTTPS" ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2017-01" certificate_arn = aws_acm_certificate.main.arn }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_lb_listener" "http" { load_balancer_arn = aws_lb.main.arn port = 80 protocol = "HTTP" # Unencrypted traffic }
Remediation: Configure HTTPS listeners, redirect HTTP to HTTPS

Description: Restrict security group rules to required ports and sources
Do: Specify exact ports and source CIDR blocks
Don't: Use 0.0.0.0/0 for all ports or allow all traffic
Code Example Good: resource "aws_security_group_rule" "https" { type = "ingress" from_port = 443 to_port = 443 protocol = "tcp" cidr_blocks = ["10.0.0.0/8"] security_group_id = aws_security_group.web.id }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_security_group_rule" "all" { type = "ingress" from_port = 0 to_port = 65535 protocol = "-1" cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"] security_group_id = aws_security_group.web.id }
Remediation: Review and restrict security group rules to specific ports/CIDRs

Description: Place sensitive resources in private subnets
Do: Use private subnets for databases, app servers, internal services
Don't: Place databases or sensitive workloads in public subnets
Code Example Good: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { db_subnet_group_name = aws_db_subnet_group.private.name publicly_accessible = false }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { publicly_accessible = true # Database exposed to internet }
Remediation: Move sensitive resources to private subnets, use NAT gateway for outbound

Description: Enable VPC Flow Logs for network traffic monitoring
Do: Enable flow logs for all VPCs, send to CloudWatch or S3
Don't: Run without network traffic visibility
Code Example Good: resource "aws_flow_log" "main" { iam_role_arn = aws_iam_role.flow_log.arn log_destination = aws_cloudwatch_log_group.flow_log.arn traffic_type = "ALL" vpc_id = aws_vpc.main.id }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_vpc" "main" { cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16" # No flow logs configured }
Remediation: Create flow log resource, configure destination (CloudWatch/S3)

Description: Enable CloudTrail for API activity logging
Do: Enable CloudTrail in all regions with log file validation
Don't: Operate without API audit trail
Code Example Good: resource "aws_cloudtrail" "main" { name = "main-trail" s3_bucket_name = aws_s3_bucket.cloudtrail.id include_global_service_events = true is_multi_region_trail = true enable_log_file_validation = true kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.cloudtrail.arn }
Code Example Bad: # No CloudTrail configured - no audit trail
Remediation: Create CloudTrail with multi-region and log validation enabled

Description: Enable AWS Config for resource compliance monitoring
Do: Enable Config with compliance rules for security baselines
Don't: Operate without resource compliance checking
Code Example Good: resource "aws_config_configuration_recorder" "main" { name = "main" role_arn = aws_iam_role.config.arn recording_group { all_supported = true include_global_resource_types = true } }
Code Example Bad: # No AWS Config - no compliance monitoring
Remediation: Enable AWS Config recorder, configure managed rules

Description: Block public access on S3 buckets by default
Do: Enable all public access blocks at account and bucket level
Don't: Allow public access without explicit business need
Code Example Good: resource "aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block" "block" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id block_public_acls = true block_public_policy = true ignore_public_acls = true restrict_public_buckets = true }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" { bucket = "my-bucket" } # No public access block - potentially public
Remediation: Add aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block resource

Description: Enable versioning on S3 buckets for data protection
Do: Enable versioning for important buckets, configure lifecycle
Don't: Disable versioning for buckets with important data
Code Example Good: resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "versioning" { bucket = aws_s3_bucket.data.id versioning_configuration { status = "Enabled" } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" { bucket = "my-bucket" } # No versioning - data loss risk
Remediation: Enable versioning, configure lifecycle for old versions

Description: Enable encryption for RDS instances
Do: Enable storage encryption with KMS key
Don't: Create unencrypted database instances
Code Example Good: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { identifier = "my-db" storage_encrypted = true kms_key_id = aws_kms_key.rds.arn }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { identifier = "my-db" storage_encrypted = false }
Remediation: Enable storage_encrypted, specify kms_key_id

Description: Enable automated backups with appropriate retention
Do: Set backup retention period to meet recovery requirements
Don't: Disable automated backups or use minimal retention
Code Example Good: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { backup_retention_period = 30 backup_window = "03:00-04:00" delete_automated_backups = false }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { backup_retention_period = 0 # Backups disabled }
Remediation: Set backup_retention_period > 0, configure backup window

Description: Never expose RDS instances publicly
Do: Set publicly_accessible = false, use private subnets
Don't: Make database instances publicly accessible
Code Example Good: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { publicly_accessible = false db_subnet_group_name = aws_db_subnet_group.private.name }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_db_instance" "db" { publicly_accessible = true # Exposed to internet }
Remediation: Set publicly_accessible = false, move to private subnet

Description: Enable EKS cluster logging for audit and debugging
Do: Enable all log types: api, audit, authenticator, controllerManager, scheduler
Don't: Run without control plane logging
Code Example Good: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { enabled_cluster_log_types = ["api", "audit", "authenticator", "controllerManager", "scheduler"] }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { # No logging configured }
Remediation: Add enabled_cluster_log_types with all types

Description: Enable envelope encryption for Kubernetes secrets
Do: Configure encryption_config with KMS key
Don't: Store secrets unencrypted in etcd
Code Example Good: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { encryption_config { provider { key_arn = aws_kms_key.eks.arn } resources = ["secrets"] } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { # No encryption config - secrets stored in plaintext }
Remediation: Add encryption_config block with KMS key

Description: Restrict EKS API server endpoint access
Do: Enable private endpoint, disable or restrict public endpoint
Don't: Expose cluster API publicly without restrictions
Code Example Good: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { vpc_config { endpoint_private_access = true endpoint_public_access = false } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_eks_cluster" "main" { vpc_config { endpoint_public_access = true public_access_cidrs = ["0.0.0.0/0"] } }
Remediation: Enable endpoint_private_access, restrict public_access_cidrs

Description: Place Lambda functions in VPC for private resource access
Do: Configure VPC config when accessing private resources
Don't: Access private resources without VPC configuration
Code Example Good: resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" { vpc_config { subnet_ids = aws_subnet.private[*].id security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.lambda.id] } }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" { # No VPC config - cannot access private resources }
Remediation: Add vpc_config with private subnets and security group

Description: Set reserved concurrency to prevent resource exhaustion
Do: Configure reserved_concurrent_executions based on capacity
Don't: Allow unlimited concurrency without protection
Code Example Good: resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" { reserved_concurrent_executions = 100 }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_lambda_function" "api" { # Unlimited concurrency - potential cost/DoS risk }
Remediation: Set reserved_concurrent_executions based on expected load

Description: Enable automatic key rotation for KMS keys
Do: Enable key rotation for symmetric CMKs
Don't: Disable key rotation or never rotate keys
Code Example Good: resource "aws_kms_key" "main" { enable_key_rotation = true deletion_window_in_days = 30 }
Code Example Bad: resource "aws_kms_key" "main" { enable_key_rotation = false }
Remediation: Set enable_key_rotation = true

Description: Enable GuardDuty for threat detection
Do: Enable GuardDuty in all regions
Don't: Operate without threat detection capabilities
Code Example Good: resource "aws_guardduty_detector" "main" { enable = true finding_publishing_frequency = "FIFTEEN_MINUTES" datasources { s3_logs { enable = true } kubernetes { audit_logs { enable = true } } } }
Code Example Bad: # No GuardDuty - no threat detection
Remediation: Create aws_guardduty_detector resource

Description: Enable Security Hub for security findings aggregation
Do: Enable Security Hub with compliance standards
Don't: Operate without centralized security visibility
Code Example Good: resource "aws_securityhub_account" "main" {} resource "aws_securityhub_standards_subscription" "cis" { standards_arn = "arn:aws:securityhub:::ruleset/cis-aws-foundations-benchmark/v/1.2.0" }
Code Example Bad: # No Security Hub - fragmented security view
Remediation: Create aws_securityhub_account and enable standards
